description: Bhandari Pariwar family members data structure. Each member has bilingual names (en/ne), gender, dates, and family relationship links.

field_descriptions.id: Unique identifier for the member. Use descriptive IDs or let Firestore auto-generate them.
field_descriptions.name: Object with 'en' (English) and 'ne' (Nepali) keys for bilingual names.
field_descriptions.birthDate: ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) or null if unknown
field_descriptions.deathDate: ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD) or null if alive/unknown
field_descriptions.isAlive: true if the member is alive, false if deceased
field_descriptions.parentId: ID of the father/parent member. null for root ancestors. Children link to the male parent. Spouses have parentId as null.
field_descriptions.spouseId: ID of the spouse member. Both spouses should point to each other.
field_descriptions.birthOrder: 0-based order among siblings (0 = eldest). Used to sort children left-to-right in the tree.
field_descriptions.photoUrl: URL to the member's photo in Firebase Storage, or null if no photo.

instructions.adding_a_couple: Create two members (male and female) and set each one's spouseId to the other's id.
instructions.adding_children: Set the child's parentId to the father's (male parent's) id. Set birthOrder to position among siblings.
instructions.root_ancestors: The topmost couple has parentId: null for both. They are linked via spouseId.
instructions.importing_to_firestore: Each member object in the 'members' array becomes a document in the 'members' collection in Firestore. The 'id' field becomes the document ID.
